# feat(dataflow): Phase 5.12 — wire FabricMetrics + Prometheus handler

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `f486da5280e3` — feat(dataflow): Phase 5.12 — wire FabricMetrics + Prometheus handler

**Body**:

Replaces the FabricMetrics no-op stub with a process-wide singleton
that every fabric subsystem dispatches through. Adds the missing
metric families (cache_errors, cache_degraded, webhook_received,
leader_status), wires every recorder, and exposes the registry over
HTTP at GET /fabric/metrics for Prometheus scrape.

metrics.py:
- Singleton lifecycle via get_fabric_metrics() / reset_fabric_metrics()
- Dedicated CollectorRegistry so tests can rebuild without colliding
  with prometheus_client's process-wide default
- 13 metric families covering pipeline, cache, source, request,
  webhook, and leader (cache_errors_total + cache_degraded + leader
  status + webhook_received are new this phase)
- render_exposition() / get_metrics_route() return Prometheus text
  exposition under a fabric-style route dict the runtime registers
  through Nexus alongside serving / health / webhook routes
- Loud no-op fallback (single startup WARN, plain-text endpoint
  message) when prometheus-client is missing — silent degradation
  to None is BLOCKED per rules/dependencies.md

cache.py:
- _record_cache_error and _set_cache_degraded dispatch to the
  singleton (in addition to the existing structured log lines so
  tests can still assert via caplog when prometheus is absent)

pipeline.py:
- get_cached records cache_hit_total / cache_miss_total and updates
  product_age_seconds gauge on every hit
- execute_product records pipeline_runs_total + pipeline_duration on
  success, error, and size-limit failure paths

serving.py:
- _make_product_handler wraps each handler with _record_request_metrics
  so request_total{product, freshness} + request_duration_seconds are
  recorded for every served product. Reads the X-Fabric-Freshness
  header so the metric label matches the user-facing freshness
  contract; tags exceptions with freshness=error

runtime.py:
- start() materializes the FabricMetrics singleton before any
  subsystem creation so cache + pipeline + leader records land on
  the same registry
- Records leader_status gauge after election so Prometheus shows
  which replica holds the lock
- _register_with_nexus appends the metrics route alongside serving,
  health, and webhook routes — operators can scrape /fabric/metrics
  immediately after db.start(nexus=...)

pyproject.toml:
- Add prometheus-client>=0.20 to the fabric extra (was previously
  resolved transitively, violating rules/dependencies.md "Declared =
  Imported")

Tests (8 new in tests/unit/fabric/test_metrics_phase_5_12.py):
- Singleton same-instance / reset semantics
- Exposition format round-trip
- Cache error counter increment via _record_cache_error
- Cache degraded gauge flip via _set_cache_degraded
- get_cached hit/miss + product_age side effects
- /fabric/metrics route shape and handler body

Total: 229 passing (up from 219 baseline). The /fabric/metrics
endpoint is now the canonical observability surface for the fabric
runtime, closing the last open todo from Phase 5 (todo-5.12).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
